channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India Reviews Meta's Response to WhatsApp Usernames Feature; Rollout Delayed
item.description: The Indian government is reviewing Meta's response to concerns about WhatsApp's proposed username feature, which allows users to create unique identifiers without revealing phone numbers. The Ministry...

item.title: Tanzania makes progress in forest restoration despite ongoing challenges
item.description: Tanzania has successfully restored 3.3 million hectares of degraded forests and landscapes as of December 2024, marking significant progress toward its environmental goals under the African Forest Lan...

item.title: Government and SARU Clash Over BEE Regulations for Springbok Tour
item.description: The South African government is proposing measures to protect the Rugby's Greatest Rivalry 2026 Tour from counterfeit goods and unauthorized businesses by designating it a protected event. This would ...

item.title: POCSO Case Backlog in Telangana: Over Half Remain Pending Trial Despite Legal Framework
item.description: The article highlights the severe backlog of Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) cases in Telangana, where over half of the registered cases remain pending trial. Between January 2016 ...

item.title: Sex Pistols' 'Filthy Lucre Live' album reissued with bonus tracks
item.description: The Sex Pistols have announced the reissue of their 1996 live album 'Filthy Lucre Live', marking 30 years since the band's unexpected reunion. The album, which captured their performance at London's F...

item.title: The 1963 Studebaker Avanti R2: A Pioneering American Sports Car Design
item.description: The 1963 Studebaker Avanti R2 stands as one of the most innovative American cars of the 1960s, blending futuristic design with advanced engineering. Designed by Raymond Loewy’s team, the Avanti featur...

item.title: Africa's football milestones at the World Cup through the years
item.description: This article traces Africa's journey in the men's football World Cup, highlighting key moments that reshaped the continent's role in global soccer. From Egypt's historic 1934 debut to Morocco's 2022 s...

item.title: Charges against accused GFL shootings suspect temporarily stayed by Crown
item.description: The Crown has temporarily halted charges against Ilan Philosophe, the founder of Astro Excavating Inc., who is accused of coordinating shootings at the homes of two executives linked to GFL Environmen...

item.title: First Nations Leaders Express Concern Over Lack of Consultation on Pipeline Announcements
item.description: First Nations leaders across Canada have expressed significant concern over the lack of consultation regarding recent pipeline announcements in Alberta, British Columbia, and Ontario. Chiefs from thes...

item.title: Half-Filipino Actor Portrays Young Zuko in ‘Avatar: The Last Airbender’
item.description: Alyana De Guzman, a Half-Filipino actor, has captured the hearts of audiences as Young Zuko in the popular series ‘Avatar: The Last Airbender.’ The role, which requires portraying the character’s emot...

item.title: Court Dismisses Jesam Michael's Attempt to Cancel Judgment in AAS Investment Fraud Case
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court in Abuja has dismissed Jesam Michael's bid to void a judgment won by 276 victims of the AAS Investment Scheme. Justice A.A. Fashola ruled that the judgme...

item.title: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito's Retirement Plans and Legal Influence
item.description: The article discusses Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito's potential retirement plans and his significant influence on the conservative legal movement. According to the Wall Street Journal, Alito has ...

item.title: Spain Advances to World Cup Semis After Late Merino Goal
item.description: In a thrilling FIFA World Cup quarter-final, Spain secured a dramatic 2-1 victory over Belgium with a last-minute goal from substitute Mikel Merino. The match at SoFi Stadium saw Spain take an early l...

item.title: Corporate Practices and Public Service Disparities
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding PrimeWater, a water utility company in Bulacan, Philippines, where corporate interests appear to overshadow public welfare. After repeated failed atte...

item.title: Balochistan Security Operations Result in 75 Terrorist Killings Since July 5
item.description: Balochistan Chief Minister Sarfraz Bugti announced that 75 terrorists have been eliminated in intelligence-based operations across the province since July 5. The statement was made during a social med...

item.title: Readers Discuss Ocean Conservation, SME Reforms, and Football Integrity
item.description: The article highlights three key topics: ocean conservation, SME reforms, and football integrity. It begins by emphasizing the urgent need to address marine pollution, particularly ghost fishing gear,...

item.title: Helabet Expands Online Gaming Offerings with Casino Games
item.description: Helabet has launched a comprehensive online casino platform, expanding its services to include a wide range of casino games for investors and gaming enthusiasts in East Africa. This move marks a signi...

item.title: The Shift from 'the United States' to 'America' and the Expansion of U.S. Colonialism
item.description: The article explores the historical transformation of the United States' national identity, shifting from 'the United States' to 'America' in 1898 due to rapid colonial expansion. This change coincide...

item.title: Sinner and Zverev Advance to Wimbledon Final
item.description: The article reports that Italian tennis player Jannik Sinner and German player Alexander Zverev have advanced to the Wimbledon final. This achievement highlights their strong performance in the tourna...
